title: veronica hectorii subsp. demissa
intro: Veronica hectorii subsp. demissa, also known as the Greek heath, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include being a diuretic and having anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Veronica hectorii subsp. demissa include treating urinary tract infections, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
